prompt: Camera orbit left. At the base of a dark basalt headland, a broad tide pool opens beside a narrow crescent beach scattered with wet stones and ribbons of kelp. Incoming swells fold around the rock wall and break into white spray, sending ripples across the clear shallows where submerged pebbles and sand bars remain visible beneath a shifting mirror of sky. Reflections of low amber light glide over the water surface while foam gathers in curved eddies along the shoreline. Farther out, the sea deepens to slate blue, and a faint mist hangs where waves strike the cliffs.

Camera orbit right. A high alpine meadow lies below a chain of snow-dusted peaks, the ground patched with late autumn grass, frost-dark soil, and a few shallow puddles left by recent sleet. Low clouds drag across the ridgeline while a break in the weather opens near the horizon, letting cold golden light rake across the valley floor. The sky transitions from bruised blue-gray storm cover to a pale luminous band, and distant snowfall remains visible over one side of the range. Wind presses through sparse firs and sends fine veils of spindrift from the upper slopes.

Camera pan left. An immense desert escarpment dominates the scene, its layered sandstone walls cut into terraces, alcoves, and vertical joints that reveal thousands of years of erosion. In front of the cliff, a field of talus slopes down to a dry wash braided with pale gravel bars and cracked mud. Isolated buttes rise beyond the main wall, softened by heat haze, while the low sun emphasizes the geometry of strata with long shadows inside creases and overhangs. Sparse shrubs cling to ledges and the air carries a faint curtain of windblown dust through the open basin.

Camera pan right. A glacial lake stretches beneath steep granite slopes, its near shore edged with rounded boulders, reeds, and a strip of wet black sand. The water is exceptionally clear at the margin, revealing stones, drowned branches, and a gentle drop into turquoise depth before it turns steel blue toward the center. Small wind-driven waves lap against the shoreline, creating broken reflections of the mountains and a line of bright highlights that flicker across the surface. At the far end, meltwater spills from a narrow cascade, raising a fine plume of spray that drifts into the cool morning air.
